remarks.name: Tigera Blog (Calico / AI Security)
remarks.role: AI agent governance, security, accountability frameworks, enterprise AI deployment policy.
remarks.text: Five Principles of an Accountable AI Agent Network: How to Evaluate Any Governance Platform
remarks.content: The first post in this series argued that AI agent governance hasn’t kept pace with deployment. The second laid out the five pillars of accountability, and what is required. The third walked through why network... The post Five Principles of an Accountable AI Agent Network: How to Evaluate Any
remarks.contentTranslation: 本系列的第一篇文章认为，AIAgent治理没有跟上部署的步伐。第二部分列出了问责制的五大支柱，以及需要做些什么。第三部分讲解了为什么要建立网络……《负责任的AIAgent网络的五项原则：如何评估
remarks.translation: 负责任的AIAgent网络的五大原则：如何评估任何治理平台
remarks.observation: 这条言论可作为 Agent 产品形态、协作方式或能力边界的观察入口；只代表公开观点，不作为公司事实主证据。

remarks.role: AI agent governance, security, accountability frameworks, enterprise AI deployment policy.
remarks.text: A field guide to the agents in your cluster
remarks.content: You know every service in your cluster by name. You know which team owns each one, what it talks to, how it scales, where its logs go. The agents are a different story. That’s not... The post A field guide to the agents in your cluster appeared first on Tigera – Creator of Calico. ]]>
remarks.contentTranslation: 您可以通过名称了解集群中的每个服务。您知道哪个团队拥有每个团队，它谈论什么，它如何扩展，它的日志在哪里。经纪人是另一回事。这不是......帖子集群中特工的现场指南首先出现在Tigera – Calico的创建者。]] >

remarks.name: Dataiku Blog
remarks.role: AI governance, enterprise AI platform deployment, MLOps, analytics, agent orchestration.
remarks.text: Enterprise AI transformation: how leading organizations are redesigning operations around AI
remarks.content: Most enterprises have run AI pilots. But few have transformed their operations with AI at scale. What separates them is organizational design, not technology: disconnected people, disconnected systems, disconnected governance. According to "Global AI confessions report: data lead
remarks.contentTranslation: 大多数企业都运行AI试点。但很少有人用AI大规模地改变了他们的运营。将它们区分开来的是组织设计，而不是技术：断开的人员、断开的系统、断开的治理。根据《全球AI认罪报告：数据线索
remarks.translation: 企业AI转型：领先组织如何围绕AI重新设计运营
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 builder 对产品、创业、销售或市场节奏的判断；可作为选题线索，不直接等同于行业结论。

remarks.text: From partnership to pipeline: how Snowflake and Dataiku drove 200%+ YoY sourced opportunity growth
remarks.content: Partnerships are often judged too early, or on the wrong things. Visibility. Executive alignment. Event presence. A well-worded press release. None of that is the real test.
remarks.contentTranslation: 人们往往过早地判断伙伴关系，或者判断错误。可见性。高管一致性。事件存在。措辞良好的新闻稿。这些都不是真正的考验。
remarks.translation: 从合作伙伴关系到渠道： Snowflake和Dataiku如何推动200% +同比机会增长

remarks.name: Dataiku Blog
remarks.role: AI governance, enterprise AI platform deployment, MLOps, analytics, agent orchestration.
remarks.text: Alteryx data analytics in 2026: strengths, limitations, and alternatives
remarks.content: In our view, Alteryx built its reputation on a simple promise: Let business analysts do data work that used to require SQL, Python, or a request to IT. For years, that promise held. Drag-and-drop workflows replaced manual spreadsheet processes. Teams that waited weeks for data engineerin
remarks.contentTranslation: 我们认为， Alteryx的声誉建立在一个简单的承诺之上：让业务分析师做过去需要SQL、Python或IT请求的数据工作。多年来，这个承诺一直保持着。拖放工作流取代了手动电子表格流程。等待数据工程师数周的团队
remarks.translation: 2026年Alteryx数据分析：优势、局限性和替代方案

remarks.name: Aaron Levie
remarks.role: ceo box - your business lives in content. unleash it with AI
remarks.text:
Lots of evidence of huge jumps in capability for Fable across coding (and related) tasks. It’s also a major jump in accuracy and success in complex knowledge work tasks.

In our Box AI Complex Work Eval, we tested the model against Opus 4.8 and saw huge boosts across almost every industry. For our eval we give the Box AI Agent, using Fable, a set of hard real world knowledge work problems that deal with enterprise documents. Then score how the agent performs the tasks.

The main differentiators for Fable vs Opus 4.8 is that it doesn't take shortcuts on complex reasoning, it gets multi-step calculations right, and it's significantly more consistent across runs. We saw the biggest leaps in Media & Entertainment (78% vs 61%), Technology (81% vs 73%), Financial Services (89% vs 83%), and Healthcare (66% vs 60%).

Here are some specific examples:

* Legal M&A due diligence: On a task reviewing NDA terms against a semiconductor company's contracting policy, Fable correctly identified that a joint-ownership clause violates exclusivity requirements while a liability cap is permitted under a Super Cap exception. Fable scored 100% vs Opus's 78%.

* Healthcare: On a clinical radiology error audit across 12 reports, Fable precisely categorized each error by severity grade and correctly concluded no Grade 3 errors existed. Opus prematurely escalated a case to "major error requiring immediate departmental review" when the evidence didn't support it — Fable 63% vs Opus 41%.

* Media & Entertainment: On a genre profitability projection task, Fable correctly recognized that a 20% Argentine tax deduction was already embedded in the source spreadsheet figures and didn't double-apply it. Opus applied it again on top — a compounding error across 4 genre calculations that took its score negative on the task vs Fable's 74%.

* Retail analytics: On a task analyzing high-growth product articles against an investment benchmark, Fable correctly computed each article's growth rate individually and identified that only 2 of 5 exceeded the threshold. Opus confused "high growth relative to average" with "above the benchmark" — scoring 61% vs Fable's 94%.

* Financial Services: On a 5-year debt facility projection, Fable correctly applied interest to opening balances and used the right capex figure. Opus applied interest to the total facility amount and computed tax from the wrong base — two compounding errors. Fable scored 83% vs Opus's 62%.

* Technology: On a SaaS feature valuation requiring computation of a Feature Value Index across multiple regions, Fable applied the formula correctly and got exact values for the markets. Opus got the arithmetic wrong on multiple criteria — Fable scored 100% vs Opus's 74%.

Overall, huge step change in complex analysis, work that requires analytical reasoning, and deep domain understanding. Fable will be available shortly in the Box AI Studio for customers to build agents with.
remarks.translation:
大量证据表明， Fable在编码（和相关）任务方面的能力大幅提升。这也是复杂知识工作任务的准确性和成功的重大飞跃。在我们的Box AI Complex Work Eval中，我们针对Opus 4.8测试了该模型，几乎在每个行业都看到了巨大的提升。对于我们的评估，我们使用Fable为Box AI Agent提供了一组处理企业文档的艰难的现实世界知识工作问题。
然后对客服代表执行任务的方式进行评分。寓言与作品4.8的主要区别在于，它不需要复杂的推理捷径，它可以正确地进行多步计算，并且在运行过程中更加一致。我们在媒体和娱乐（ 78%对61% ）、技术（ 81%对73% ）、金融服务（ 89%对83% ）和医疗保健（ 66%对60% ）方面实现了最大的飞跃。
以下是一些具体示例： *法律并购尽职调查：在根据半导体公司的合同政策审查保密协议条款的任务中， Fable正确地识别出共同所有权条款违反了排他性要求，而超级上限例外允许责任上限。寓言的得分为100% ，而Opus的得分为78%。
*医疗保健：在对12份报告的临床放射学错误审核中， Fable按严重程度对每个错误进行了精确分类，并正确得出不存在3级错误的结论。当证据不支持时， Opus提前将个案升级为“需要立即进行部门审查的重大错误” —寓言63% vs Opus 41%。
*媒体和娱乐：在一项类型盈利能力预测任务中，寓言正确地认识到， 20%的阿根廷税收减免已经嵌入到源电子表格数据中，并且没有重复应用。Opus再次将其应用于顶部— 4种流派计算的复合误差，在任务中得分为负，而寓言的得分为74 ％。
*零售分析：在一项根据投资基准分析高增长产品文章的任务中， Fable正确地单独计算了每篇文章的增长率，并确定只有2/5超过了阈值。Opus将“相对于平均水平的高增长”与“高于基准”混为一谈--得分为61% ，而Fable得分为94%。
*金融服务：在5年期债务融资预测中， Fable正确地将利息应用于期初余额，并使用了正确的资本支出数字。OPUS将利息应用于总设施金额和来自错误基数的计算税收—两个复利误差。寓言的得分为83% ，而Opus的得分为62%。
*技术：在需要跨多个地区计算特征值指数的SaaS特征估值中， Fable正确应用了公式并获得了市场的确切值。Opus在多个标准上都算错了算术--Fable的得分为100% ，而Opus的得分为74%。总体而言，在复杂的分析、需要分析推理的工作和深刻的领域理解方面发生了巨大的变化。
Fable将很快在Box AI Studio中提供，供客户构建Agent。
remarks.observation: 这条言论可作为 Agent 产品形态、协作方式或能力边界的观察入口；只代表公开观点，不作为公司事实主证据。

remarks.name: Zara Zhang
remarks.role: Builder. Dangerously skips permissions. Harvard’17. GitHub: https://t.co/KCuEajezlL YouTube: https://t.co/8xzbGWtf6w
remarks.text:
People should build agents/skills for their cross-functional teams.

For example, if a design team builds a design agent/skill for the marketing team (trained on all of the brand's guidelines and design patterns), then the marketing team can produce more on-brand assets without having to bug designers every time

But the marketing team couldn't have built this on their own; it takes the designer with their expertise, context, and knowledge

Same thing applies to every pair of teams that work closely with each other, often rely on each other, and complain about each other's limited bandwidth

Building agents for your cross-functional teams ensures each team can be more self-sufficient, and moves us to a direction where teams can be organized by "loops" rather than "functions"
remarks.translation:
员工应为其跨职能团队建立Agent/技能。
例如，如果设计团队为营销团队构建设计Agent/技能（接受过所有品牌指南和设计模式的培训） ，那么营销团队可以制作更多的品牌资产，而无需每次都对设计师进行错误分析。但营销团队不可能自己构建设计Agent/技能；这需要设计师的专业知识、背景和知识。同样的事情也适用于彼此密切合作的每一对团队，通常依赖于每个团队
并抱怨彼此的带宽有限为您的跨职能团队构建Agent可确保每个团队能够更加自给自足，并将我们推向一个团队可以按“循环”而不是“职能”组织的方向
remarks.observation: 这条言论可作为 Agent 产品形态、协作方式或能力边界的观察入口；只代表公开观点，不作为公司事实主证据。

remarks.text:
It seems like most startups in San Francisco are selling products to each other

When I ask founders who their target audience is, 90% of the time it's "engineering and product teams, AI-native startups"

Feels like the same small group of target audience is being bombarded with a million products, whereas very few people are building for the 99% of the world

remarks.name: Matt Turck
remarks.role: VC at FirstMarkCap.  Host: MAD Podcast; Organizer: Data Driven NYC, Author: MAD Landscape.
remarks.text: 2026 is a BRUTAL grind in VC. You start in Davos, freeze in Aspen, hit Upfront, survive Milken, then it’s straight to Paris for the French Open.  Briefly back in NYC for the Knicks. Then, total blur: SuperReturn in Berlin, Founders Forum in London, then back stateside for the World Cup, back to Paris for Raise AI, Idaho for Sun Valley, quick respite in Mykonos, then the Goldman tech gauntlet, Slush in Finland, NeurIPS in freakin’ Sydney… and *boom*, a productive year of thought leadership and adding value is over, and you’re a wreck.
remarks.translation:
2026年是VC的残酷磨难。您从达沃斯出发，在阿斯彭冻结，提前击中，在米尔肯生存，然后直接前往巴黎参加法网公开赛。短暂回到纽约尼克斯队。
然后，完全模糊：柏林的SuperReturn ，伦敦的Founders Forum ，然后回到美国参加世界杯，回到巴黎的Raise AI ，爱达荷州的Sun Valley ，米科诺斯的快速喘息，然后是高盛的科技挑战赛，芬兰的Slush ，悉尼的NeurIPS……还有* BOOM * ，一个富有成效的思想领导力和增加价值的一年已经结束，你是一个残骸。
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 builder 对产品、创业、销售或市场节奏的判断；可作为选题线索，不直接等同于行业结论。

remarks.name: Peter Yang
remarks.role: Practical AI tutorials and interviews to help busy people become AI builders
remarks.text:
Give yourself permission to build.

The traditional career ladder pushes everyone to become a leader, but I just want to be a builder.

As you climb the ladder at most companies, you’re expected to step away from building and fill your time with product reviews, cross-functional alignment, managing up, and performance calibrations.

I know a lot of builders who spent their best years climbing the wrong ladder.

The good news is that this is finally changing. Companies are rewarding builders and ICs more than ever, and even managers are increasingly expected to do IC work too.

But becoming a good builder takes reps, and it’s hard to put in those reps when you’re in back-to-back meetings all day.

So if you’re a builder at heart, embrace it. You don’t have to give up what you’re good at to be a “leader.”

📌 Watch now: https://t.co/wv2SINll18
remarks.translation:
允许自己构建。传统的职业阶梯推动每个人成为领导者，但我只想成为一名建筑师。当您在大多数公司攀登梯子时，您需要远离构建，并用产品评论、跨职能调整、管理和绩效校准来填补您的时间。我认识很多建筑工人，他们花了最好的时间爬错了梯子。好消息是，这种情况终于发生了变化。
公司对建筑商和集成电路的回报比以往任何时候都高，甚至管理人员也越来越多地被期望从事集成电路工作。但成为一名优秀的建设者需要代表，当你整天背靠背开会时，很难安排这些代表。因此，如果您本质上是一名建筑师，请拥抱它。你不必为了成为“领导者”而放弃自己擅长的事情。”立即📌观看：
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 builder 对产品、创业、销售或市场节奏的判断；可作为选题线索，不直接等同于行业结论。

remarks.name: Madhu Guru
remarks.role: Prev: Product Leader at Google - Gemini, Veo, Nano Banana.
remarks.text:
Right from the early days of Gemini, enterprises would get the quality/cost tradeoff wrong. They’d often start with the smallest, cheapest model.

The rule of thumb we gave customers:

Replacing a traditional ML model with an LLM - start small, cos you already know what good looks like.

Building something new - start with the most capable model. Think magically. Figure out what’s actually possible first.

Once they had a high-quality working application, we’d help them move to a smaller model while maintaining quality.
remarks.translation:
从双子座的早期开始，企业就会错误地权衡质量/成本。他们通常从最小、最便宜的型号开始。我们给客户的经验法则是：用LLM取代传统的机器学习模型--从小事做起，因为你已经知道什么是好的。构建新事物-从最有能力的模型开始。神奇地思考。首先弄清楚什么是真正可能的。
一旦他们拥有高质量的工作应用程序，我们将帮助他们转向更小的模型，同时保持质量。
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察模型运行、算力、存储、sandbox 或开发平台基础设施的变化；后续事实判断需回到原始公告或技术文档。

remarks.contentTranslation: AI爱好者正在与时间赛跑，AI怀疑论者正在与熵慈善专业赛跑，他们巧妙地捕捉到AI爱好者和AI怀疑论者之间的动态，他们都在尝试
remarks.translation: AI 乐观派在与时间赛跑，AI 怀疑派在与熵赛跑
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 builder 对产品、创业、销售或市场节奏的判断；可作为选题线索，不直接等同于行业结论。

podcasts.name: Unsupervised Learning (Redpoint)
podcasts.title: Ep 89: AI Research Legend’s Honest Assessment of Where We Are
podcasts.excerpt: This episode with Lukasz Kaiser, co-author of the seminal "Attention Is All You Need" transformer paper and former researcher at both Google Brain and OpenAI, is a wide-ranging conversation about the fundamental limits of current AI architectures and whether transformers will continue to dominate or eventually give way to something new. Lukasz brings a rare dual perspective: deep belief in how far the current paradigm has taken us (he's an enthusiastic daily Codex user who's seen 10x productivity gains in his own research), while maintaining genuine intellectual humility about whether transformers can truly generalize the way humans do. The episode weaves together questions about data efficiency, the non-verifiable RL frontier, the coding agent revolution, the open vs. closed source gap, and what the next architectural leap might look like: all filtered through the lens of someone who helped bu…

podcasts.name: Latent Space
podcasts.title: 🔬ESM: The Bitter Lesson is Coming for Proteins - Alex Rives, BioHub
podcasts.excerpt: Editor’s note: In our first BioHub pod with Priscilla and Mark they discussed their acquisition of EvoScale, led by Alex Rives, who is now Head of Science at BioHub. With ESM-1 they trained language models on millions of protein sequences drawn from across life, with a simple “next token” objective: predict the amino acids that have been randomly masked out, based on the context of the rest of the sequence. But they soon found that these models also learned biological structure and function, including properties the model had never been explicitly s…

podcasts.name: AI & I by Every
podcasts.title: We Automated Everything With AI and Tripled Our Headcount
podcasts.excerpt: Dan Shipper runs one of the most AI-native companies today. Every has agents embedded in nearly every workflow—“if you swing a stick in our Slack, you're as likely to hit a human as an agent,” he says. And yet the company has grown from four people to 30 since GPT-3 came out, and is still hiring.Why does Dan believe there's more human work to do than ever?In a format flip for AI & I, Every's COO Brandon Gell turns the tables and interviews Dan about his latest essay, “After Automation”—an 8,000-word argument for why rising automation doesn't eliminate demand for human work, it increases it. The thesis: AI makes yesterday's expert competence cheap and widely available, which floods every field with output that's close but not quite right—and that creates more demand for the humans who can take it the rest of the way.Dan talked with Brandon about the paradox at the heart of a…
